patterns.title: Missing signer authorization
patterns.why_it_matters: Authority-like AccountInfo fields without Signer or #[account(signer)] let callers spoof the authority account.
look_for: token transfer authorities used without signer checks
patterns.fix: Use Signer<'info>, #[account(signer)], or an explicit is_signer/require check before privileged actions.

patterns.title: Missing account owner validation
patterns.why_it_matters: Raw AccountInfo data can be deserialized from accounts owned by other programs unless owner is checked first.
patterns.fix: Use Account<'info, T> or require the account owner to equal the expected program id before reading or writing.

patterns.title: Account data matching / type cosplay
patterns.why_it_matters: Programs must bind deserialized account data to the expected discriminator, owner, and semantic identity.
look_for: missing discriminator or key equality checks
patterns.fix: Use Anchor Account<T> or explicitly validate discriminator, owner, and expected keys before trusting fields.

patterns.title: Arbitrary CPI target
patterns.why_it_matters: If a CPI program id comes from user-controlled accounts, attackers can route trusted account metas to malicious programs.
look_for: missing equality check against known program id
patterns.fix: Use Program<'info, Token> or require_keys_eq!(program.key(), spl_token::ID) before invoking.

patterns.title: Non-canonical or shared PDA derivation
patterns.why_it_matters: User-controlled bumps or insufficient seed domains can validate alternate PDAs or share authority across users.
patterns.fix: Use canonical bumps and Anchor seeds/bump constraints with domain-separated seeds.

patterns.title: Uninitialized or re-initializable account
patterns.why_it_matters: Manual initialization without an initialized flag or discriminator check can allow repeated initialization.
patterns.fix: Use Anchor init constraints or write and enforce an initialized discriminator before mutation.

patterns.title: Duplicate mutable accounts
patterns.why_it_matters: Two writable accounts may alias the same address unless the program enforces distinctness.
patterns.fix: Add require_keys_neq! or Anchor account constraints to enforce distinct writable accounts.

patterns.title: Sysvar address spoofing
patterns.why_it_matters: Sysvar accounts passed as raw AccountInfo can be spoofed unless the address or Sysvar type is enforced.
patterns.fix: Use Sysvar<'info, T> or require the account key to equal the canonical sysvar id.

patterns.title: Unsafe account closing / account revival
patterns.why_it_matters: Draining lamports without writing the closed-account discriminator can allow account revival.
patterns.fix: Use Anchor close constraints or zero data and write the closed-account discriminator after draining lamports.
